subject: aptitude
question: पेन : लिहिणे :: चाकू : ? / Pen : Writing :: Knife : ?
explanation: पेनचा उपयोग लिहिण्यासाठी होतो, तसाच चाकूचा उपयोग कापण्यासाठी होतो / A pen is used for writing, similarly a knife is used for cutting.

subject: aptitude
question: डॉक्टर : रुग्णालय :: शिक्षक : ? / Doctor : Hospital :: Teacher : ?
explanation: डॉक्टर रुग्णालयात काम करतो, तसाच शिक्षक शाळेत काम करतो / A doctor works in a hospital, similarly a teacher works in a school.

subject: aptitude
question: मुंबई : महाराष्ट्र :: चेन्नई : ? / Mumbai : Maharashtra :: Chennai : ?
explanation: मुंबई ही महाराष्ट्राची राजधानी आहे, तशीच चेन्नई ही तमिळनाडूची राजधानी आहे / Mumbai is the capital of Maharashtra, similarly Chennai is the capital of Tamil Nadu.

subject: aptitude
question: मधमाशी : पोळे :: कोळी : ? / Bee : Hive :: Spider : ?
explanation: मधमाशी पोळ्यात राहते, तसाच कोळी जाळ्यात राहतो / A bee lives in a hive, similarly a spider lives in a web.

subject: aptitude
explanation: प्रत्येक संख्येचा वर्ग घेतला आहे (5²=25), त्यामुळे 7²=49 / Each number is squared (5²=25), so 7²=49.

subject: aptitude
question: गाय : वासरू :: मांजर : ? / Cow : Calf :: Cat : ?
explanation: गायीच्या पिल्लाला वासरू म्हणतात, तसेच मांजरीच्या पिल्लाला 'पिल्लू (kitten)' म्हणतात / A baby cow is called a calf, similarly a baby cat is called a kitten.

subject: aptitude
question: पुस्तक : लेखक :: चित्र : ? / Book : Author :: Painting : ?
explanation: पुस्तक लेखक तयार करतो, तसेच चित्र चित्रकार तयार करतो / A book is created by an author, similarly a painting is created by a painter.

subject: aptitude
question: थर्मामीटर : तापमान :: बॅरोमीटर : ? / Thermometer : Temperature :: Barometer : ?
explanation: थर्मामीटरने तापमान मोजतात, तसेच बॅरोमीटरने वायुदाब मोजतात / A thermometer measures temperature, similarly a barometer measures air pressure.

subject: aptitude
question: गोदावरी : महाराष्ट्र :: कावेरी : ? / Godavari : Maharashtra :: Kaveri : ?
explanation: गोदावरी नदी महाराष्ट्रातून उगम पावते, तशीच कावेरी नदी कर्नाटकातून (कूर्गमधून) उगम पावते / The Godavari river originates in Maharashtra, similarly the Kaveri river originates in Karnataka.

subject: aptitude
question: AB : DE :: GH : ? / AB : DE :: GH : ?
explanation: प्रत्येक अक्षर मालिकेत दोन अक्षरे पुढे सरकतात (A→D, B→E म्हणजे +3), त्यामुळे G→J, H→K / Each pair shifts three letters forward (A→D, B→E), so G→J, H→K giving JK.

subject: aptitude
question: कवी : कविता :: शिल्पकार : ? / Poet : Poem :: Sculptor : ?
explanation: कवी कविता निर्माण करतो, तसेच शिल्पकार मूर्ती निर्माण करतो / A poet creates a poem, similarly a sculptor creates a sculpture.

subject: aptitude
question: लोकसभा : संसद सदस्य :: विधानसभा : ? / Lok Sabha : MP :: Vidhan Sabha : ?
explanation: लोकसभेचा सदस्य खासदार (MP) असतो, तसेच विधानसभेचा सदस्य आमदार (MLA) असतो / A member of the Lok Sabha is called an MP, similarly a member of the Vidhan Sabha is called an MLA.

subject: aptitude
question: एका ठराविक सांकेतिक भाषेत, जर CAT ला DBU असे लिहितात (प्रत्येक अक्षर एका पुढील अक्षराने बदलले आहे), तर त्याच सांकेतिक भाषेत DOG कसे लिहितील? / In a certain code language, if CAT is written as DBU (each letter shifted one place forward), how is DOG written in that code?
explanation: Shifting each letter one place forward: D→E, O→P, G→H gives EPH.

subject: aptitude
question: एका सांकेतिक भाषेत BOOK ला 2-15-15-11 असे लिहितात (अक्षरांचे वर्णमालेतील स्थान वापरून), तर PEN कसे लिहतील? / In a code, BOOK is written as 2-15-15-11 (using the alphabetical position of each letter). How is PEN written?
explanation: P=16, E=5, N=14, so PEN is coded as 16-5-14.

subject: aptitude
question: जर MADRAS ला NBESBT असे लिहितात (प्रत्येक अक्षर एक स्थान पुढे सरकवले आहे), तर BOMBAY कसे लिहतील? / If MADRAS is coded as NBESBT (each letter shifted one place forward), how is BOMBAY coded?
explanation: Shifting each letter of BOMBAY forward by one gives C-P-N-C-B-Z, i.e., CPNCBZ.

subject: aptitude
question: एका सांकेतिक भाषेत A=Z, B=Y, C=X अशा उलट क्रमाने अक्षरे बदलली जातात. या नियमानुसार CAT कसे लिहतील? / In a code, letters are replaced in reverse order: A=Z, B=Y, C=X, and so on. Following this rule, how is CAT coded?
explanation: Using the rule position n → (27−n): C(3)→X(24), A(1)→Z(26), T(20)→G(7), giving XZG.

subject: aptitude
question: एका सांकेतिक भाषेत, प्रत्येक अक्षर वर्णमालेत विरुद्ध क्रमांकाच्या अक्षराने (A↔Z, B↔Y...) बदलले जाते, त्यामुळे GARDEN ला TZIWVM असे लिहितात. याच नियमानुसार FLOWER कसे लिहतील? / In a code, each letter is replaced by its mirror letter in the alphabet (A↔Z, B↔Y, ...), so GARDEN becomes TZIWVM. Using the same rule, how is FLOWER coded?
explanation: Applying n→(27−n) to F,L,O,W,E,R gives U,O,L,D,V,I, i.e., UOLDVI.

subject: aptitude
question: जर TABLE ला UBCMF असे लिहितात (प्रत्येक अक्षर एक स्थान पुढे सरकवले आहे), तर CHAIR कसे लिहतील? / If TABLE is coded as UBCMF (each letter shifted forward by one), how is CHAIR coded?
explanation: Shifting each letter of CHAIR forward by one gives D-I-B-J-S, i.e., DIBJS.

subject: aptitude
question: जर PAPER ला QBQFS असे लिहितात, तर PENCIL कसे लिहतील? / If PAPER is coded as QBQFS, how is PENCIL coded?
explanation: Shifting each letter of PENCIL forward by one gives Q-F-O-D-J-M, i.e., QFODJM.

subject: aptitude
question: जर ROSE या शब्दाचा कोड 57 असेल (प्रत्येक अक्षराच्या वर्णमाला-स्थानांची बेरीज), तर LOTUS चा कोड काय असेल? / If the code for ROSE is 57 (sum of the alphabetical positions of its letters), what is the code for LOTUS?

subject: aptitude
question: एका सांकेतिक भाषेत 'ink is red' असे 'pit na oo' लिहितात, 'chalk is white' असे 'la oo fu' लिहितात, आणि 'chalk and ink' असे 'la ce pit' लिहितात. तर 'white' साठी कोड काय असेल? / In a code language, 'ink is red' is written as 'pit na oo', 'chalk is white' is written as 'la oo fu', and 'chalk and ink' is written as 'la ce pit'. What is the code for 'white'?
explanation: Comparing sentences: 'is'=oo (common to sentences 1,2), 'ink'=pit (common to 1,3), 'chalk'=la (common to 2,3), leaving 'white'=fu in sentence 2.

subject: aptitude
question: जर TEACHER ला UFBDIFS असे लिहितात, तर CHILDREN कसे लिहतील? / If TEACHER is coded as UFBDIFS, how is CHILDREN coded?
explanation: Shifting each letter of CHILDREN forward by one gives D-I-J-M-E-S-F-O, i.e., DIJMESFO.

subject: aptitude
question: जर MPSC या शब्दाचा कोड (अक्षरांची वर्णमाला-स्थाने वापरून) 13-16-19-3 असेल, तर SSC या शब्दाचा कोड काय असेल? / If the code for MPSC (using alphabetical positions of letters) is 13-16-19-3, what is the code for SSC?
explanation: S=19, S=19, C=3, so SSC is coded as 19-19-3.

subject: aptitude
question: एका सांकेतिक भाषेत प्रत्येक अक्षर (27 वजा त्याचे वर्णमाला-स्थान) या नियमाने बदलले जाते, त्यामुळे INDIA ला 18-13-23-18-26 असे लिहितात. याच नियमानुसार PUNE कसे लिहतील? / In a code, each letter is replaced using the rule (27 minus its alphabetical position), so INDIA is coded as 18-13-23-18-26. Using the same rule, how is PUNE coded?

subject: aptitude
question: एक व्यक्ती उत्तरेकडे ५ किमी चालतो, नंतर उजवीकडे वळून ५ किमी चालतो, आणि पुन्हा उजवीकडे वळून ५ किमी चालतो. तो सुरुवातीच्या ठिकाणापासून किती अंतरावर व कोणत्या दिशेला आहे? / A man walks 5 km towards North, then turns right and walks 5 km, then again turns right and walks 5 km. How far and in which direction is he from his starting point?
explanation: Plotting the path on coordinates (North then East then South) shows the final point lies 5 km due East of the starting point.

subject: aptitude
question: एक व्यक्ती १० मीटर उत्तरेकडे, नंतर १० मीटर पूर्वेकडे आणि नंतर १० मीटर दक्षिणेकडे चालतो. तो आता सुरुवातीच्या ठिकाणापासून कोणत्या दिशेला व किती अंतरावर आहे? / A person walks 10m North, then 10m East, then 10m South. Where is he now with respect to the starting point?
explanation: The North and South displacements of 10m each cancel out, leaving only the 10m Eastward displacement.

subject: aptitude
question: एका छायाचित्राकडे बोट दाखवत एक पुरुष म्हणाला, "ती माझ्या आजोबांच्या एकुलत्या एक मुलाची मुलगी आहे." त्या स्त्रीचा त्या पुरुषाशी काय संबंध आहे? / Pointing to a photograph, a man said, "She is the daughter of my grandfather's only son." How is the woman related to the man?
explanation: The grandfather's only son (since the speaker is a grandson, not a son) is the speaker's father, so his daughter is the speaker's sister.

subject: aptitude
question: एका स्त्रीची ओळख करून देताना एक पुरुष म्हणाला, "तिची आई माझ्या आईची एकुलती एक मुलगी आहे." त्या पुरुषाचा त्या स्त्रीशी काय संबंध आहे? / Introducing a woman, a man said, "Her mother is the only daughter of my mother." How is the man related to the woman?
explanation: Since the speaker is male, his mother's only daughter must be his sister, and her daughter's maternal uncle is the speaker.

subject: aptitude
question: रवी दक्षिणेकडे ६ किमी चालतो, नंतर डावीकडे वळून ३ किमी चालतो, आणि पुन्हा डावीकडे वळून ६ किमी चालतो. तो आता सुरुवातीच्या ठिकाणापासून किती अंतरावर व कोणत्या दिशेला आहे? / Ravi walks 6 km towards South, then turns left and walks 3 km, then again turns left and walks 6 km. How far and in which direction is he now from the starting point?
explanation: Facing South and turning left twice makes him face North again; the two 6 km South-then-North legs cancel, leaving only the 3 km Eastward leg.

subject: aptitude
question: A आणि B हे भाऊ आहेत. C आणि D या बहिणी आहेत. A चा मुलगा D चा भाऊ आहे. B चा C शी काय संबंध आहे? / A and B are brothers. C and D are sisters. A's son is D's brother. What is B's relation to C?
explanation: Since A's son is D's brother, A is the father of C and D; B, being A's brother, is therefore the paternal uncle of C.

subject: aptitude
question: एका सकाळी सुरेश सूर्याकडे पाठ करून चालू लागला. काही अंतर चालल्यानंतर तो डावीकडे वळला आणि नंतर पुन्हा उजवीकडे वळला. आता तो कोणत्या दिशेला चालत आहे? / One morning, Suresh started walking with his back towards the sun. After walking some distance, he turned to his left, then again turned to his right. In which direction is he walking now?
explanation: In the morning the sun rises in the East, so having his back to it means Suresh initially faces West; a left turn then a right turn cancel each other, so he still faces West.

subject: aptitude
question: एका पुरुषाकडे बोट दाखवत एक स्त्री म्हणाली, "त्याची आई माझ्या वडिलांची एकुलती एक मुलगी आहे." त्या स्त्रीचा त्या पुरुषाशी काय संबंध आहे? / Pointing to a man, a woman said, "His mother is the only daughter of my father." How is the woman related to the man?
explanation: Since the speaker is a woman, her father's only daughter is the speaker herself, meaning she is the man's mother.

subject: aptitude
question: एक पर्यटक पुणे रेल्वे स्थानकावरून पूर्वेकडे ८ किमी चालून एका मंदिरात पोहोचतो. नंतर तो उजवीकडे वळून ६ किमी चालून एका बाजारपेठेत पोहोचतो. रेल्वे स्थानक आणि बाजारपेठ यामधील कमीत कमी अंतर किती आणि बाजारपेठ स्थानकापासून कोणत्या दिशेला आहे? / A tourist starts from Pune railway station, walks 8 km East to reach a temple, then turns right and walks 6 km to reach a market. What is the shortest distance between the station and the market, and in which direction is the market from the station?
explanation: The 8 km East and 6 km South legs form a right angle, so by the Pythagorean theorem the straight-line distance is 10 km, and the market lies in the South-East direction from the station.

subject: aptitude
question: दीपक म्हणाला, "ती मुलगी माझ्या आईच्या एकुलत्या एक नातवाची पत्नी आहे." दीपकचा त्या मुलीशी काय संबंध आहे? / Deepak said, "She is the wife of my mother's only grandson." How is Deepak related to the girl?
explanation: The mother's only grandson is Deepak's own son, so the girl who is his son's wife is Deepak's daughter-in-law, making Deepak her father-in-law.

subject: aptitude
question: राहुल उत्तरेकडे १५ मीटर चालतो. नंतर उजवीकडे वळून १० मीटर चालतो. पुन्हा उजवीकडे वळून १५ मीटर चालतो. नंतर डावीकडे वळून ५ मीटर चालतो. तो सुरुवातीच्या ठिकाणापासून किती अंतरावर व कोणत्या दिशेला आहे? / Rahul walks 15m towards North. He then turns right and walks 10m. He again turns right and walks 15m. He then turns left and walks 5m. How far and in which direction is he from his starting position?
explanation: Tracking the coordinates step by step (North, East, South, East) shows the North and South legs of 15m cancel, leaving a net 15m displacement due East.

subject: aptitude
question: P हा Q चा भाऊ आहे. R ही P ची आई आहे. S हा R चा वडील आहे. T हा S चा भाऊ आहे. Q चा T शी काय संबंध आहे? / P is the brother of Q. R is the mother of P. S is the father of R. T is the brother of S. How is Q related to T?
options: B. पणतू आजोबा (आजोबांचा भाऊ) / Grand-uncle (grandfather's brother)
explanation: R is the mother of both P and Q, S (R's father) is their grandfather, and T, being S's brother, is therefore Q's grand-uncle.

subject: aptitude
question: संख्येत 20% वाढ करून, नंतर मिळालेल्या संख्येत 20% घट केली, तर मूळ संख्येच्या तुलनेत निव्वळ बदल किती? / A number is increased by 20% and then the resulting number is decreased by 20%. What is the net percentage change compared to the original number?
options: C. कोणताही बदल नाही / No change
explanation: 100 चे 1.2 पट करून नंतर 0.8 पट केल्यास 96 मिळते, म्हणजे मूळ संख्येपेक्षा 4% घट होते / Taking 100 → ×1.2 → ×0.8 gives 96, a net 4% decrease.

subject: aptitude
question: दोन संख्यांचे गुणोत्तर 3:5 आहे आणि त्यांची बेरीज 96 आहे, तर मोठी संख्या कोणती? / Two numbers are in the ratio 3:5 and their sum is 96. Find the larger number.
explanation: एकूण 8 भाग = 96, म्हणजे 1 भाग = 12, मोठी संख्या = 5×12 = 60 / Total 8 parts = 96, so 1 part = 12, and the larger number = 5×12 = 60.

subject: aptitude
question: A एक काम 12 दिवसांत आणि B तेच काम 18 दिवसांत पूर्ण करतो. दोघांनी एकत्र काम केल्यास ते किती दिवसांत पूर्ण होईल? / A can complete a work in 12 days and B can complete the same work in 18 days. In how many days will they complete it together?
explanation: 1/12 + 1/18 = 5/36, त्यामुळे एकत्र काम पूर्ण होण्यास 36/5 = 7.2 दिवस लागतील / 1/12 + 1/18 = 5/36, so together they take 36/5 = 7.2 days.

subject: aptitude
question: एका निवडणुकीत विजयी उमेदवाराला 60% मते मिळाली आणि तो 4000 मतांनी विजयी झाला. एकूण मतदान किती झाले? / In an election, the winning candidate received 60% of the votes and won by a margin of 4000 votes. What was the total number of votes polled?
explanation: मताधिक्य हे एकूण मतांच्या 20% इतके आहे, त्यामुळे 20% = 4000 म्हणून एकूण मते = 20000 / The margin equals 20% of total votes, so 20% = 4000, giving total votes = 20000.

subject: aptitude
question: 2400 रुपये A, B, C मध्ये 2:3:5 या गुणोत्तरात वाटले असता C चा वाटा किती? / Rs. 2400 is divided among A, B and C in the ratio 2:3:5. What is C's share?

subject: aptitude
question: A आणि B एकत्र एक काम 10 दिवसांत पूर्ण करतात, तर A एकटा तेच काम 15 दिवसांत पूर्ण करतो. या कामासाठी मिळालेले 3000 रुपये कामाच्या प्रमाणात वाटल्यास B चा वाटा किती? / A and B together complete a work in 10 days, while A alone completes it in 15 days. If Rs. 3000 is divided between them in proportion to the work done, what is B's share?
explanation: A चा दर = 1/15, एकत्रित दर = 1/10, म्हणून B चा दर = 1/10 - 1/15 = 1/30; A:B = 2:1, त्यामुळे B ला (1/3)×3000 = 1000 रुपये मिळतील / A's rate = 1/15, combined rate = 1/10, so B's rate = 1/30; ratio A:B = 2:1, giving B a share of (1/3)×3000 = Rs. 1000.

subject: aptitude
question: एका दुकानदाराने 2500 रुपये किमतीच्या वस्तूवर सलग 10% आणि 20% सूट दिली. वस्तूची विक्री किंमत किती? / A shopkeeper offers successive discounts of 10% and 20% on an item marked at Rs. 2500. What is the selling price?

subject: aptitude
question: A ची कार्यक्षमता B च्या दुप्पट आहे. दोघे मिळून एक काम 12 दिवसांत पूर्ण करतात, तर A एकटा ते काम किती दिवसांत पूर्ण करेल? / A is twice as efficient as B. Together they complete a work in 12 days. In how many days will A alone complete the work?
explanation: B चा दर x असल्यास A चा दर 2x आहे; एकत्रित दर 3x = 1/12, म्हणून A चा दर = 2x = 1/18, म्हणजे A ला एकट्याला 18 दिवस लागतील / If B's rate is x, A's rate is 2x; combined rate 3x = 1/12, so A's rate = 1/18, meaning A alone takes 18 days.

subject: aptitude
question: एका जिल्हा परिषदेच्या परीक्षेत मुले व मुली यांचे गुणोत्तर 5:4 असून एकूण 540 उमेदवार उपस्थित होते. मुलींची संख्या किती? / In a Zilla Parishad exam, the ratio of boys to girls among candidates is 5:4, and a total of 540 candidates appeared. How many girls appeared?
explanation: एकूण भाग = 9, प्रत्येक भाग = 60, मुली = 4×60 = 240 / Total parts = 9, each part = 60, so girls = 4×60 = 240.

subject: aptitude
question: एका गावाची लोकसंख्या पहिल्या वर्षी 10% ने वाढली आणि दुसऱ्या वर्षी 10% ने घटली. सध्याची लोकसंख्या 9900 असल्यास मूळ लोकसंख्या किती होती? / The population of a village increased by 10% in the first year and decreased by 10% in the second year. If the present population is 9900, what was the original population?
explanation: मूळ लोकसंख्या P असल्यास P×1.1×0.9 = 0.99P = 9900, म्हणून P = 10000 / If original population is P, then P×1.1×0.9 = 0.99P = 9900, so P = 10000.

subject: aptitude
question: नळ A एक टाकी 6 तासांत भरतो आणि नळ B तीच टाकी 8 तासांत रिकामी करतो. दोन्ही नळ एकाच वेळी रिकाम्या टाकीत उघडले, तर टाकी किती तासांत भरेल? / Pipe A can fill a tank in 6 hours and pipe B can empty it in 8 hours. If both pipes are opened together on an empty tank, in how many hours will the tank be filled?
explanation: निव्वळ दर = 1/6 - 1/8 = 1/24, त्यामुळे टाकी 24 तासांत भरेल / Net rate = 1/6 - 1/8 = 1/24, so the tank fills in 24 hours.

subject: aptitude
question: दूध आणि पाणी यांचे मिश्रण 4:1 या गुणोत्तरात असून एकूण मिश्रण 25 लिटर आहे. हे गुणोत्तर 4:3 करण्यासाठी किती लिटर पाणी मिसळावे लागेल? / A mixture of milk and water is in the ratio 4:1, with a total of 25 litres. How many litres of water must be added to change the ratio to 4:3?
explanation: दूध = 20 लिटर (स्थिर), सुरुवातीचे पाणी = 5 लिटर; 4:3 गुणोत्तरासाठी नवीन पाणी = 15 लिटर, म्हणजे 10 लिटर पाणी मिसळावे लागेल / Milk = 20 L (constant), initial water = 5 L; for ratio 4:3, new water must be 15 L, so 10 L of water must be added.

subject: aptitude
question: पुढील मालिकेतील रिकाम्या जागी कोणती संख्या येईल? 4, 8, 12, 16, ?, 24 / Find the missing number in the series: 4, 8, 12, 16, ?, 24
explanation: ही मालिका 4 च्या पटीत आहे, म्हणून पुढील संख्या 20 आहे. / The series consists of multiples of 4, so the missing number is 20.

subject: aptitude
question: पुढील मालिकेतील रिकाम्या जागी कोणती संख्या येईल? 3, 9, 27, 81, ? / Find the missing number in the series: 3, 9, 27, 81, ?
explanation: प्रत्येक संख्येला 3 ने गुणल्यास पुढील संख्या मिळते, म्हणजे 81 × 3 = 243. / Each term is multiplied by 3, so 81 × 3 = 243.

subject: aptitude
question: पुढील मालिकेतील रिकाम्या जागी कोणती संख्या येईल? 7, 14, 21, 28, ?, 42 / Find the missing number in the series: 7, 14, 21, 28, ?, 42
explanation: ही मालिका 7 च्या पटीत आहे, म्हणून रिकाम्या जागी 35 येईल. / The series is multiples of 7, so the missing number is 35.

subject: aptitude
question: पुढील मालिकेतील रिकाम्या जागी कोणती संख्या येईल? 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, ? / Find the missing number in the series: 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, ?
explanation: ही मालिका सलग नैसर्गिक संख्यांच्या वर्गांची आहे (1², 2², 3²...), म्हणून पुढील संख्या 6² = 36 आहे. / The series consists of squares of consecutive natural numbers, so the next term is 6² = 36.

subject: aptitude
question: पुढील मालिकेतील रिकाम्या जागी कोणती संख्या येईल? 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, ? / Find the missing number in the series: 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, ?
explanation: ही मालिका मूळ संख्यांची (prime numbers) आहे, म्हणून 11 नंतरची मूळ संख्या 13 आहे. / The series is of prime numbers, and the prime following 11 is 13.

subject: aptitude
question: पुढील मालिकेतील रिकाम्या जागी कोणती संख्या येईल? 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, ? / Find the missing number in the series: 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, ?
explanation: ही फिबोनाची मालिका आहे, प्रत्येक संख्या आधीच्या दोन संख्यांची बेरीज असते, म्हणून 5+8=13. / This is the Fibonacci series where each term is the sum of the previous two, so 5+8=13.

subject: aptitude
question: पुढील मालिकेतील रिकाम्या जागी कोणती संख्या येईल? 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ? / Find the missing number in the series: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ?
explanation: फरक 4, 6, 8, 10 असे 2 ने वाढत जातात (n²+n प्रकार), त्यामुळे पुढील फरक 12 असून उत्तर 30+12=42 आहे. / The differences increase by 2 each time (4,6,8,10), following n²+n, so the next difference is 12, giving 30+12=42.

subject: aptitude
question: पुढील मालिकेतील रिकाम्या जागी कोणती संख्या येईल? 5, 11, 19, 29, 41, ? / Find the missing number in the series: 5, 11, 19, 29, 41, ?
explanation: फरक 6, 8, 10, 12 असे 2 ने वाढत जातात, त्यामुळे पुढील फरक 14 असून उत्तर 41+14=55 आहे. / The differences increase by 2 each time (6,8,10,12), so the next difference is 14, giving 41+14=55.

subject: aptitude
question: पुढील मालिकेतील रिकाम्या जागी कोणती संख्या येईल? 3, 8, 15, 24, 35, ? / Find the missing number in the series: 3, 8, 15, 24, 35, ?
explanation: फरक 5, 7, 9, 11 असे 2 ने वाढत जातात (n²+2n प्रकार), त्यामुळे पुढील फरक 13 असून उत्तर 35+13=48 आहे. / The differences increase by 2 each time (5,7,9,11), following n²+2n, so the next difference is 13, giving 35+13=48.

subject: aptitude
question: पुढील मालिकेतील रिकाम्या जागी कोणती संख्या येईल? 2, 5, 10, 17, 26, ? / Find the missing number in the series: 2, 5, 10, 17, 26, ?
explanation: प्रत्येक संख्या n²+1 (n=1,2,3...) या नियमाने येते, त्यामुळे 6²+1=37 हे उत्तर आहे. / Each term follows n²+1, so for n=6, the value is 6²+1=37.

subject: aptitude
question: पुढील मालिकेतील रिकाम्या जागी कोणती संख्या येईल? 6, 11, 21, 36, 56, ? / Find the missing number in the series: 6, 11, 21, 36, 56, ?
explanation: फरक 5, 10, 15, 20 असे 5 च्या पटीत वाढत जातात, त्यामुळे पुढील फरक 25 असून उत्तर 56+25=81 आहे. / The differences increase in multiples of 5 (5,10,15,20), so the next difference is 25, giving 56+25=81.

subject: aptitude
question: पुढील मालिकेतील रिकाम्या जागी कोणती संख्या येईल? 3, 9, 7, 13, 11, ?, 15 / Find the missing number in the series: 3, 9, 7, 13, 11, ?, 15
explanation: ही मालिका आलटून-पालटून +6 आणि -2 या क्रियांनी तयार होते, म्हणजे 11+6=17. / The series alternates between adding 6 and subtracting 2, so 11+6=17.

subject: aptitude
question:
विधाने: सर्व मांजरी कुत्री आहेत. सर्व कुत्री पक्षी आहेत.
निष्कर्ष: I. सर्व मांजरी पक्षी आहेत. II. काही पक्षी मांजरी आहेत.
/ Statements: All cats are dogs. All dogs are birds.
Conclusions: I. All cats are birds. II. Some birds are cats.
options: A. फक्त निष्कर्ष I बरोबर आहे / Only conclusion I follows
B. फक्त निष्कर्ष II बरोबर आहे / Only conclusion II follows
C. दोन्ही निष्कर्ष I आणि II बरोबर आहेत / Both conclusions I and II follow
D. दोन्हीपैकी कोणताही निष्कर्ष बरोबर नाही / Neither conclusion follows
explanation: Since all cats are dogs and all dogs are birds, all cats are birds (I), and since cats are a subset of birds, some birds are cats (II); both follow.

subject: aptitude
question:
विधाने: कोणताही दगड धातू नाही. सर्व धातू खनिजे आहेत.
निष्कर्ष: I. कोणतेही खनिज दगड नाही. II. काही खनिजे दगड नाहीत.
/ Statements: No stone is a metal. All metals are minerals.
Conclusions: I. No mineral is a stone. II. Some minerals are not stones.
options: A. फक्त निष्कर्ष I बरोबर आहे / Only conclusion I follows
B. फक्त निष्कर्ष II बरोबर आहे / Only conclusion II follows
C. दोन्ही निष्कर्ष I आणि II बरोबर आहेत / Both conclusions I and II follow
D. दोन्हीपैकी कोणताही निष्कर्ष बरोबर नाही / Neither conclusion follows
explanation: Since metals (which are not stones) are a subset of minerals, some minerals are definitely not stones, but it cannot be concluded that no mineral at all is a stone.

subject: aptitude
question:
विधाने: काही पेन पुस्तके आहेत. सर्व पुस्तके कागद आहेत.
निष्कर्ष: I. काही पेन कागद आहेत. II. काही कागद पेन आहेत.
/ Statements: Some pens are books. All books are papers.
Conclusions: I. Some pens are papers. II. Some papers are pens.
options: A. फक्त निष्कर्ष I बरोबर आहे / Only conclusion I follows
B. फक्त निष्कर्ष II बरोबर आहे / Only conclusion II follows
C. दोन्ही निष्कर्ष I आणि II बरोबर आहेत / Both conclusions I and II follow
D. दोन्हीपैकी कोणताही निष्कर्ष बरोबर नाही / Neither conclusion follows
explanation: Some pens being books which are all papers gives some pens are papers (I), and by simple conversion of this I-type statement, some papers are pens (II); both follow.

subject: aptitude
question:
विधाने: सर्व डॉक्टर अभियंता आहेत. कोणताही अभियंता शेतकरी नाही.
निष्कर्ष: I. कोणताही डॉक्टर शेतकरी नाही. II. काही डॉक्टर शेतकरी नाहीत.
/ Statements: All doctors are engineers. No engineer is a farmer.
Conclusions: I. No doctor is a farmer. II. Some doctors are not farmers.
options: A. फक्त निष्कर्ष I बरोबर आहे / Only conclusion I follows
B. फक्त निष्कर्ष II बरोबर आहे / Only conclusion II follows
C. दोन्ही निष्कर्ष I आणि II बरोबर आहेत / Both conclusions I and II follow
D. दोन्हीपैकी कोणताही निष्कर्ष बरोबर नाही / Neither conclusion follows
explanation: Since all doctors are engineers and no engineer is a farmer, no doctor is a farmer (I), and since this universal negative holds, some doctors are not farmers (II) also follows.

subject: aptitude
question:
विधाने: काही शिक्षक लेखक आहेत. काही लेखक चित्रकार आहेत.
निष्कर्ष: I. काही शिक्षक चित्रकार आहेत. II. कोणताही शिक्षक चित्रकार नाही.
/ Statements: Some teachers are writers. Some writers are painters.
Conclusions: I. Some teachers are painters. II. No teacher is a painter.
options: A. फक्त निष्कर्ष I बरोबर आहे / Only conclusion I follows
B. फक्त निष्कर्ष II बरोबर आहे / Only conclusion II follows
C. दोन्ही निष्कर्ष I आणि II बरोबर आहेत / Both conclusions I and II follow
D. दोन्हीपैकी कोणताही निष्कर्ष बरोबर नाही / Neither conclusion follows
explanation: Two particular (some) premises with no common distributed term yield no definite conclusion about teachers and painters, so neither conclusion follows.

subject: aptitude
question:
विधाने: सर्व आंबे फळे आहेत. काही फळे गोड आहेत.
निष्कर्ष: I. काही आंबे गोड आहेत. II. काही गोड पदार्थ आंबे आहेत.
/ Statements: All mangoes are fruits. Some fruits are sweet.
Conclusions: I. Some mangoes are sweet. II. Some sweet things are mangoes.
options: A. फक्त निष्कर्ष I बरोबर आहे / Only conclusion I follows
B. फक्त निष्कर्ष II बरोबर आहे / Only conclusion II follows
C. दोन्ही निष्कर्ष I आणि II बरोबर आहेत / Both conclusions I and II follow
D. दोन्हीपैकी कोणताही निष्कर्ष बरोबर नाही / Neither conclusion follows
explanation: The 'some fruits are sweet' may refer entirely to fruits other than mangoes, so neither that some mangoes are sweet nor that some sweet things are mangoes can be validly concluded.

subject: aptitude
question:
विधाने: सर्व खोल्या खिडक्या आहेत. काही खिडक्या दरवाजे आहेत.
निष्कर्ष: I. काही दरवाजे खोल्या आहेत. II. कोणताही दरवाजा खोली नाही.
/ Statements: All rooms are windows. Some windows are doors.
Conclusions: I. Some doors are rooms. II. No door is a room.
options: A. फक्त निष्कर्ष I बरोबर आहे / Only conclusion I follows
B. फक्त निष्कर्ष II बरोबर आहे / Only conclusion II follows
C. एकतर निष्कर्ष I किंवा II बरोबर आहे / Either conclusion I or II follows
D. दोन्हीपैकी कोणताही निष्कर्ष बरोबर नाही / Neither conclusion follows
explanation: Neither conclusion follows individually since the 'some windows are doors' may or may not overlap with rooms, but as the two conclusions are complementary (some vs none) and jointly exhaust all possible cases, either I or II must follow.

subject: aptitude
question:
विधाने: सर्व पोपट पक्षी आहेत. सर्व चिमण्या पक्षी आहेत.
निष्कर्ष: I. सर्व पोपट चिमण्या आहेत. II. काही पक्षी पोपट आहेत.
/ Statements: All parrots are birds. All sparrows are birds.
Conclusions: I. All parrots are sparrows. II. Some birds are parrots.
options: A. फक्त निष्कर्ष I बरोबर आहे / Only conclusion I follows
B. फक्त निष्कर्ष II बरोबर आहे / Only conclusion II follows
C. दोन्ही निष्कर्ष I आणि II बरोबर आहेत / Both conclusions I and II follow
D. दोन्हीपैकी कोणताही निष्कर्ष बरोबर नाही / Neither conclusion follows
explanation: Two universal statements sharing the same predicate (birds) do not establish any relation between parrots and sparrows, but since all parrots are birds, some birds are indeed parrots.

subject: aptitude
question:
विधाने: कोणतेही टेबल खुर्ची नाही. काही खुर्च्या लाकडी वस्तू आहेत.
निष्कर्ष: I. काही लाकडी वस्तू टेबल नाहीत. II. कोणतीही लाकडी वस्तू टेबल नाही.
/ Statements: No table is a chair. Some chairs are wooden items.
Conclusions: I. Some wooden items are not tables. II. No wooden item is a table.
options: A. फक्त निष्कर्ष I बरोबर आहे / Only conclusion I follows
B. फक्त निष्कर्ष II बरोबर आहे / Only conclusion II follows
C. दोन्ही निष्कर्ष I आणि II बरोबर आहेत / Both conclusions I and II follow
D. दोन्हीपैकी कोणताही निष्कर्ष बरोबर नाही / Neither conclusion follows
explanation: Since no table is a chair and some chairs are wooden items, those wooden items that are chairs are certainly not tables, giving 'some wooden items are not tables', but a blanket 'no wooden item is a table' cannot be guaranteed as other wooden items could be tables.

subject: aptitude
question:
विधाने: काही विद्यार्थी खेळाडू आहेत. कोणताही खेळाडू आळशी नाही.
निष्कर्ष: I. काही विद्यार्थी आळशी नाहीत. II. कोणताही विद्यार्थी आळशी नाही.
/ Statements: Some students are athletes. No athlete is lazy.
Conclusions: I. Some students are not lazy. II. No student is lazy.
options: A. फक्त निष्कर्ष I बरोबर आहे / Only conclusion I follows
B. फक्त निष्कर्ष II बरोबर आहे / Only conclusion II follows
C. दोन्ही निष्कर्ष I आणि II बरोबर आहेत / Both conclusions I and II follow
D. दोन्हीपैकी कोणताही निष्कर्ष बरोबर नाही / Neither conclusion follows
explanation: Since some students are athletes and no athlete is lazy, those students who are athletes are certainly not lazy, so 'some students are not lazy' follows, but it cannot be concluded that no student at all is lazy.

subject: aptitude
question:
विधाने: सर्व नद्या जलस्रोत आहेत. सर्व जलस्रोत नैसर्गिक संसाधने आहेत. सर्व नैसर्गिक संसाधने मौल्यवान आहेत.
निष्कर्ष: I. सर्व नद्या मौल्यवान आहेत. II. काही मौल्यवान गोष्टी नद्या आहेत.
/ Statements: All rivers are water bodies. All water bodies are natural resources. All natural resources are valuable.
Conclusions: I. All rivers are valuable. II. Some valuable things are rivers.
options: A. फक्त निष्कर्ष I बरोबर आहे / Only conclusion I follows
B. फक्त निष्कर्ष II बरोबर आहे / Only conclusion II follows
C. दोन्ही निष्कर्ष I आणि II बरोबर आहेत / Both conclusions I and II follow
D. दोन्हीपैकी कोणताही निष्कर्ष बरोबर नाही / Neither conclusion follows
explanation: Chaining the three universal statements gives all rivers are valuable (I), and since rivers are thus a subset of valuable things, some valuable things are rivers (II); both follow.

subject: aptitude
question:
विधाने: सर्व फाईली फोल्डर आहेत. कोणताही फोल्डर दस्तऐवज नाही.
निष्कर्ष: I. कोणतीही फाईल दस्तऐवज नाही. II. काही फोल्डर फाईल नाहीत.
/ Statements: All files are folders. No folder is a document.
Conclusions: I. No file is a document. II. Some folders are not files.
options: A. फक्त निष्कर्ष I बरोबर आहे / Only conclusion I follows
B. फक्त निष्कर्ष II बरोबर आहे / Only conclusion II follows
C. दोन्ही निष्कर्ष I आणि II बरोबर आहेत / Both conclusions I and II follow
D. दोन्हीपैकी कोणताही निष्कर्ष बरोबर नाही / Neither conclusion follows
explanation: Since all files are folders and no folder is a document, no file can be a document (I), but the premises are equally consistent with every folder being a file, so 'some folders are not files' does not necessarily follow.

question: मार्च 2026 पासून महाराष्ट्राचे राज्यपाल म्हणून कोणाची नियुक्ती करण्यात आली आहे? / Who has been appointed as the Governor of Maharashtra since March 2026?

subject: current_affairs
question: 'मुख्यमंत्री बळीराजा मोफत वीज योजने' अंतर्गत किती अश्वशक्ती (HP) पर्यंतच्या कृषी पंपांना मोफत वीज दिली जाते? / Under the 'Chief Minister Baliraja Free Electricity Scheme', agricultural pumps up to how much horsepower (HP) get free electricity?

question: आदिवासी महिलांच्या सक्षमीकरणासाठी महाराष्ट्र शासनाने कोणती नवीन योजना सुरू केली आहे? / Which new scheme has the Maharashtra government launched for empowerment of tribal women?

subject: current_affairs
question: मराठी भाषेला अभिजात भाषेचा दर्जा कोणत्या तारखेला केंद्र सरकारने मंजूर केला, जो आता 'मराठी अभिजात भाषा दिन' म्हणून साजरा केला जातो? / On which date did the Central Government approve classical language status for Marathi, now observed as 'Marathi Abhijaat Bhasha Divas'?

subject: current_affairs
question: नाशिक-त्र्यंबकेश्वर सिंहस्थ कुंभमेळा 2026 कोणत्या तारखेला ध्वजारोहणाने सुरू होणार आहे? / On which date does the Nashik-Trimbakeshwar Simhastha Kumbh Mela 2026 begin with the traditional flag-hoisting (dhwajarohan)?

subject: current_affairs
question: छत्रपती शिवाजी महाराजांच्या राज्याभिषेकाचा 350 वा वर्धापन दिन कोणत्या किल्ल्यावर साजरा करण्यात आला? / At which fort was the 350th anniversary celebration of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj's coronation held?

subject: current_affairs
question: 2026 च्या महाराष्ट्र विधान परिषद निवडणुकीत महायुतीने एकूण किती जागांपैकी किती जागा जिंकल्या? / In the 2026 Maharashtra Legislative Council (Vidhan Parishad) elections, how many seats out of the total did the Mahayuti alliance win?

subject: current_affairs
question: खेलो इंडिया युथ गेम्स 2025 मध्ये सर्वाधिक पदकांसह अव्वल स्थान कोणत्या राज्याने पटकावले? / Which state topped the medal tally at the Khelo India Youth Games (KIYG) 2025?
explanation: Maharashtra topped the KIYG 2025 medal table with 158 medals (58 gold, 47 silver, 53 bronze), retaining its title for a fifth consecutive time.

subject: current_affairs
question: मुंबई ट्रान्स हार्बर लिंक (अटल सेतू) संबंधित उलवे कोस्टल रोड प्रकल्पाचा पहिला टप्पा कधीपर्यंत पूर्ण होणे अपेक्षित आहे? / By when is Phase 1 of the Ulwe Coastal Road project (linked to Atal Setu / Mumbai Trans Harbour Link) expected to be completed?

subject: current_affairs
question: भारताचे ५३ वे सरन्यायाधीश (CJI) म्हणून २४ नोव्हेंबर २०२५ रोजी कोणी शपथ घेतली? / Who took oath as the 53rd Chief Justice of India (CJI) on 24 November 2025?

subject: current_affairs
question: लोकसभेच्या जागा ५४३ वरून वाढवण्याचा प्रस्ताव असलेले विधेयक एप्रिल २०२६ मध्ये लोकसभेत सादर करण्यात आले, त्याचे नाव काय आहे? / The bill proposing to increase Lok Sabha seats from 543, introduced in Lok Sabha in April 2026, is called what?

subject: current_affairs
question: प्रजासत्ताक दिन २०२६ (७७ वा) निमित्त जाहीर झालेल्या पद्म पुरस्कारांमध्ये एकूण किती प्राप्तकर्त्यांचा समावेश होता? / How many total recipients were named in the Padma Awards announced on the eve of the 77th Republic Day 2026?

subject: current_affairs
question: २०२६ च्या पद्म विभूषण पुरस्कारांमध्ये मरणोत्तर सन्मानित करण्यात आलेले प्रसिद्ध अभिनेते कोण होते? / Which noted actor was honoured posthumously with the Padma Vibhushan in the 2026 list?
options: B. धर्मेंद्र सिंग देओल / Dharmendra Singh Deol
explanation: Veteran actor Dharmendra Singh Deol was honoured with the Padma Vibhushan (posthumously) in the 2026 awards list, along with former Kerala CM V. S. Achuthanandan.

subject: current_affairs
question: मार्च २०२६ मध्ये झालेल्या राज्यपाल फेरबदलामध्ये महाराष्ट्राचे नवीन राज्यपाल म्हणून कोणाची नियुक्ती झाली? / In the gubernatorial reshuffle of March 2026, who was appointed as the new Governor of Maharashtra?

explanation: Jishnu Dev Varma, previously Governor of Telangana, was transferred as the new Governor of Maharashtra from 10 March 2026 as part of a nine-state gubernatorial reshuffle.

subject: current_affairs
question: भारताची १६ वी जनगणना (Census 2027) ही देशाच्या इतिहासातील पहिली कोणत्या प्रकारची जनगणना ठरणार आहे? / India's 16th Census (Census 2027) will be historic as the country's first census of what kind?
options: A. फक्त शहरी भागांची जनगणना / Urban-areas-only census
B. पूर्णपणे डिजिटल जनगणना / Fully digital census
C. दर पाच वर्षांनी होणारी जनगणना / Census held every five years
D. फक्त आर्थिक जनगणना / Purely economic census
explanation: Census 2027 is India's first fully digital census, using mobile apps for enumerators and a self-enumeration portal in 16 languages, with Phase I (House Listing) starting 1 April 2026.

subject: current_affairs
question: Census 2027 मध्ये जातनिहाय गणना (caste enumeration) किती वर्षांनंतर प्रथमच होत आहे? / Census 2027 will include caste enumeration for the first time since which earlier census year?
explanation: Caste enumeration is being conducted in Census 2027 for the first time since the 1931 census.

subject: current_affairs
question: सप्टेंबर २०२५ पासून लागू झालेल्या GST सुधारणांनंतर बहुतांश वस्तू व सेवांसाठी मुख्य कर स्लॅब कोणते ठेवण्यात आले? / After the GST reforms effective from September 2025, what are the main simplified tax slabs for most goods and services?
explanation: The GST reforms merged the four-tier structure into mainly two slabs — 5% for essential goods and 18% for standard goods/services, with 40% for luxury/sin goods, effective 22 September 2025.

subject: current_affairs
question: GST सुधारणांनंतर आरोग्य व जीवन विमा हप्त्यांवरील GST दर किती करण्यात आला? / After the GST reforms, what is the new GST rate on health and life insurance premiums?
explanation: GST on individual health and life insurance premiums was completely removed (reduced to 0%) as part of the September 2025 GST reforms.

subject: current_affairs
question: इस्रोच्या गगनयान-१ मानवरहित चाचणी मोहिमेत कोणता अर्ध-मानवरूपी रोबोट (हॅाफ-ह्युमनॉइड) पाठवला जाणार आहे? / Which half-humanoid robot is set to be sent aboard ISRO's uncrewed Gaganyaan-1 test mission?
explanation: Vyommitra, ISRO's half-humanoid robot, will fly on the Gaganyaan-1 uncrewed mission scheduled for the second half of 2026 to simulate astronaut life-support conditions.

subject: current_affairs
question: मे २०२६ मध्ये केंद्रीय मंत्रिमंडळाने किती कोटी रुपयांच्या कोळसा वायुनिर्मिती (कोल गॅसिफिकेशन) योजनेला मंजुरी दिली? / In May 2026, the Union Cabinet approved a Coal Gasification Scheme worth how much money?
explanation: The Union Cabinet approved a ₹37,500 crore Coal Gasification Scheme in May 2026, along with the ₹20,667 crore Ahmedabad (Sarkhej)-Dholera rail project.

subject: current_affairs
question: जानेवारी 2025 मध्ये इस्रोच्या स्पॅडेक्स (SpaDeX) मोहिमेत अंतराळात दोन उपग्रहांचे यशस्वी डॉकिंग करून भारत हा असे करणारा जगातील कितवा देश ठरला? / In January 2025, ISRO's SpaDeX mission achieved successful in-space docking of two satellites, making India which country in the world to accomplish this feat?
explanation: SpaDeX docking on 16 January 2025 made India the fourth nation after the USA, Russia, and China to achieve autonomous space docking.

subject: current_affairs
question: इस्रोच्या स्पॅडेक्स मोहिमेत वापरण्यात आलेल्या स्वदेशी डॉकिंग प्रणालीचे नाव काय आहे? / What is the name of the indigenous docking system used in ISRO's SpaDeX mission?
options: A. भारतीय अंतरिक्ष प्रणाली / Bharatiya Antariksha System
B. भारतीय डॉकिंग प्रणाली / Bharatiya Docking System
C. गगन डॉकिंग यंत्रणा / Gagan Docking Mechanism
D. आदित्य डॉकिंग यंत्र / Aditya Docking Unit
explanation: The SpaDeX mission demonstrated docking using the indigenously developed 'Bharatiya Docking System', paving the way for the Bharatiya Antariksha Station and Gaganyaan.

subject: current_affairs
question: 30 जुलै 2025 रोजी प्रक्षेपित करण्यात आलेला NISAR उपग्रह हा कोणत्या दोन देशांचा संयुक्त उपक्रम आहे? / The NISAR satellite, launched on 30 July 2025, is a joint mission between which two countries?
options: A. भारत आणि रशिया / India and Russia
B. भारत आणि जपान / India and Japan
C. भारत आणि अमेरिका (नासा) / India and USA (NASA)
D. भारत आणि फ्रान्स / India and France
explanation: NISAR (NASA-ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar), launched via GSLV-F16 on 30 July 2025, is the world's first dual-frequency (L-band and S-band) radar imaging satellite, a joint ISRO-NASA mission.

subject: current_affairs
question: 18 मे 2025 रोजी इस्रोच्या PSLV-C61 प्रक्षेपकाचे अपयश कोणत्या टप्प्यात (स्टेज) झाले, ज्यामुळे EOS-09 उपग्रह गमवावा लागला? / During the 18 May 2025 PSLV-C61 launch failure that led to the loss of the EOS-09 satellite, at which stage did the anomaly occur?
explanation: A drop in chamber pressure in the PSLV's third stage caused the mission failure, only the third such failure in PSLV's 32-year history.

subject: current_affairs
question: भारताच्या राष्ट्रीय क्वांटम मिशन अंतर्गत 2026 मध्ये किती किलोमीटर अंतराचे सुरक्षित क्वांटम संदेशवहन नेटवर्क यशस्वीरित्या प्रस्थापित करण्यात आले? / Under India's National Quantum Mission, a secure quantum communication network of how many kilometres was successfully demonstrated in 2026?
explanation: India achieved a 1,000-km secure quantum communication network milestone under the National Quantum Mission using indigenous technology developed by QNu Labs.

subject: current_affairs
question: राष्ट्रीय क्वांटम मिशन अंतर्गत क्वांटम चिप व सेन्सर उत्पादनासाठी उभारण्यात येणाऱ्या दोन फॅब्रिकेशन सुविधांपैकी एक महाराष्ट्रातील कोणत्या संस्थेत उभारली जात आहे? / One of the two quantum chip and sensor fabrication facilities being set up under the National Quantum Mission is located at which Maharashtra institute?
explanation: The two quantum chip and sensor fabrication facilities under the National Quantum Mission are being established at IIT Bombay and IISc Bangalore.

subject: current_affairs
question: 2025 सालचे भौतिकशास्त्रातील नोबेल पारितोषिक कोणत्या शोधासाठी जॉन क्लार्क, मिशेल डेव्होरे आणि जॉन मार्टिनिस यांना विभागून देण्यात आले? / The 2025 Nobel Prize in Physics was jointly awarded to John Clarke, Michel Devoret, and John Martinis for discoveries related to which phenomenon?
options: B. विद्युत परिपथांमध्ये क्वांटम टनेलिंग / Quantum tunnelling in electrical circuits
explanation: They were awarded the 2025 Physics Nobel for demonstrating macroscopic quantum mechanical tunnelling and energy quantisation in an electrical circuit.

subject: current_affairs
question: 2025 सालचे रसायनशास्त्रातील नोबेल पारितोषिक सुसुमु किटागावा, रिचर्ड रॉब्सन आणि ओमर याघी यांना कोणत्या संशोधनासाठी देण्यात आले? / The 2025 Nobel Prize in Chemistry was awarded to Susumu Kitagawa, Richard Robson, and Omar Yaghi for the development of what?
options: B. mRNA लस तंत्रज्ञान / mRNA vaccine technology
C. CRISPR जनुक संपादन / CRISPR gene editing
D. सौर पेशी तंत्रज्ञान / Solar cell technology
explanation: They won the 2025 Chemistry Nobel for developing metal-organic frameworks (MOFs), porous materials used for gas storage, water harvesting and filtration.

subject: current_affairs
question: गगनयान कार्यक्रमांतर्गत मानवरहित चाचणी उड्डाण गगनयान-1 (G1) मध्ये अंतराळवीरांच्या परिस्थितीचे अनुकरण करण्यासाठी कोणता रोबोट पाठवला जाणार आहे? / Which robot will be carried on the uncrewed Gaganyaan-1 (G1) test flight to simulate astronaut conditions?
explanation: The Gaganyaan-1 uncrewed test flight will carry the half-humanoid robot Vyommitra to provide data on life-support and environmental systems ahead of crewed flights.

subject: current_affairs
question: भारताचे शेवटचे विज्ञान शाखेतील (भौतिकशास्त्र/रसायनशास्त्र/वैद्यकशास्त्र) नोबेल पारितोषिक विजेते, ज्यांनी भारतातच राहून संशोधन करून हा बहुमान मिळवला, ते कोण होते? / Who was the last Indian to win a Nobel Prize in the sciences (Physics/Chemistry/Medicine) while working entirely in India?
options: A. सी. व्ही. रामन / C.V. Raman
explanation: C.V. Raman, who discovered the Raman Effect in 1930, remains India's only Nobel laureate in sciences awarded for work done entirely within India, a gap highlighted again after the 2025 Nobel announcements.

subject: current_affairs
question: जानेवारी २०२५ मध्ये राष्ट्रपती भवनात प्रदान करण्यात आलेला 'मेजर ध्यानचंद खेलरत्न पुरस्कार' खालीलपैकी कोणत्या नेमबाजाला मिळाला? / Which shooter received the 'Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna Award' presented at Rashtrapati Bhavan in January 2025?
options: B. सिफ्त कौर सामरा / Sift Kaur Samra
explanation: Olympic bronze medallist shooter Manu Bhaker was among the recipients of the Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna Award 2024, conferred in January 2025.

subject: current_affairs
question: ८० वर्षीय युद्धवीर आणि पॅरालिम्पिक सुवर्णपदक विजेता, ज्यांना २०२५ मध्ये 'जीवनगौरव अर्जुन पुरस्कार' देण्यात आला, त्यांचे नाव काय? / Who is the 80-year-old war hero and Paralympic gold medallist honoured with the 'Arjuna Award (Lifetime)' in 2025?
explanation: Murlikant Petkar, India's first Paralympic gold medallist and a war veteran, received the lifetime Arjuna Award in the 2025 National Sports Awards ceremony.

subject: current_affairs
question: फेब्रुवारी २०२५ मध्ये अहिल्यानगर येथे झालेल्या ६७ व्या महाराष्ट्र केसरी कुस्ती स्पर्धेचा किताब कोणी जिंकला? / Who won the title of the 67th Maharashtra Kesari wrestling competition held at Ahilyanagar in February 2025?
explanation: Pune's Pruthviraj Mohol defeated Mahendra Gaikwad in the final to win the 67th Maharashtra Kesari title and the silver mace.

subject: current_affairs
question: मे २०२५ मध्ये झालेल्या ७ व्या खेलो इंडिया युथ गेम्सचे यजमानपद कोणत्या राज्याने भूषवले, ज्यात महाराष्ट्राने सलग तिसऱ्यांदा विजेतेपद (५८ सुवर्णांसह १५८ पदके) पटकावले? / Which state hosted the 7th Khelo India Youth Games in May 2025, at which Maharashtra won the overall title for the third consecutive time (158 medals including 58 golds)?
explanation: The 2025 Khelo India Youth Games were hosted by Bihar (Patna, Rajgir, Gaya, Bhagalpur, Begusarai) in May 2025, while Maharashtra topped the medal tally.

subject: current_affairs
question: २ नोव्हेंबर २०२५ रोजी नवी मुंबईतील डॉ. डी. वाय. पाटील स्टेडियमवर झालेल्या अंतिम सामन्यात दक्षिण आफ्रिकेचा पराभव करून भारताच्या महिला संघाने कोणती ऐतिहासिक स्पर्धा प्रथमच जिंकली? / By defeating South Africa in the final at Dr. DY Patil Stadium, Navi Mumbai on 2 November 2025, which historic title did the Indian women's team win for the first time?
options: A. आयसीसी महिला क्रिकेट विश्वचषक / ICC Women's Cricket World Cup
B. महिला टी-२० विश्वचषक / Women's T20 World Cup
C. आशिया चषक (महिला) / Women's Asia Cup
D. कॉमनवेल्थ गेम्स क्रिकेट स्पर्धा / Commonwealth Games cricket event
explanation: India defeated South Africa by 52 runs in the final at Navi Mumbai on 2 November 2025 to win their maiden ICC Women's Cricket World Cup title.

subject: current_affairs
question: ३ जून २०२५ रोजी अहमदाबादच्या नरेंद्र मोदी स्टेडियमवर झालेल्या अंतिम सामन्यात पंजाब किंग्जचा पराभव करून आयपीएल २०२५ चे विजेतेपद कोणत्या संघाने प्रथमच पटकावले? / Which team won its maiden IPL title by defeating Punjab Kings in the final at Narendra Modi Stadium, Ahmedabad on 3 June 2025?
options: A. चेन्नई सुपर किंग्ज / Chennai Super Kings
C. रॉयल चॅलेंजर्स बेंगळुरू / Royal Challengers Bengaluru
D. कोलकाता नाइट रायडर्स / Kolkata Knight Riders
explanation: Royal Challengers Bengaluru beat Punjab Kings by 6 runs in the IPL 2025 final to win their first-ever IPL title after 18 years.

subject: current_affairs
question: ऑक्टोबर ३१, २०२५ रोजी दिल्लीच्या थ्यागराज इनडोअर स्टेडियमवर पुणेरी पलटणचा पराभव करून प्रो कबड्डी लीग २०२५ चे विजेतेपद कोणत्या संघाने मिळवले? / Which team defeated Puneri Paltan at Delhi's Thyagaraj Indoor Stadium on 31 October 2025 to win the Pro Kabaddi League 2025 title?
options: B. दबंग दिल्ली के.सी. / Dabang Delhi K.C.
C. जयपूर पिंक पँथर्स / Jaipur Pink Panthers
explanation: Dabang Delhi KC defeated Maharashtra's Puneri Paltan 31-28 in the final to win their second Pro Kabaddi League title in the 2025 season.

subject: current_affairs
question: २०२६ मध्ये जाहीर झालेल्या पद्म पुरस्कारांमध्ये क्रीडा क्षेत्रातून महाराष्ट्राच्या कोणत्या माजी क्रिकेट कर्णधाराला पद्मश्री पुरस्काराने सन्मानित करण्यात आले? / In the Padma Awards announced in 2026, which former Indian cricket captain from Maharashtra was honoured with the Padma Shri in the sports category?
explanation: Rohit Sharma from Maharashtra was named among the Padma Shri awardees in the sports category in the Padma Awards list released in January 2026.

subject: current_affairs
question: जुलै २०२५ मध्ये विम्बल्डन पुरुष एकेरीचे विजेतेपद कार्लोस अल्काराझला हरवून कोणी पटकावले आणि विम्बल्डन एकेरी जिंकणारा पहिला इटालियन खेळाडू ठरला? / Who defeated Carlos Alcaraz to win the Wimbledon 2025 men's singles title, becoming the first Italian to win a Wimbledon singles crown?
explanation: Jannik Sinner defeated Carlos Alcaraz in the 2025 Wimbledon men's singles final, becoming the first Italian to win a Wimbledon singles title.

subject: current_affairs
question: २०२५ च्या रणजी करंडक स्पर्धेच्या अंतिम सामन्यात कर्नाटकचा पराभव करून कोणत्या संघाने आपले पहिलेवहिले विजेतेपद मिळवले? / Which team won its maiden Ranji Trophy title by defeating Karnataka in the 2025-26 final?
options: B. जम्मू आणि काश्मीर / Jammu and Kashmir
explanation: Jammu and Kashmir defeated Karnataka in the 2025-26 Ranji Trophy final to win their maiden title, qualifying for the final for the first time in their history.

subject: english
question:
Read the passage and answer the question below:
"Maharashtra has emerged as one of India's leading states in renewable energy generation, particularly wind and solar power. The state government has implemented policies to encourage private investment in solar parks and wind farms, aiming to reduce dependence on coal-based thermal power. However, experts caution that inconsistent transmission infrastructure and land acquisition disputes continue to slow down the pace of renewable energy projects. Despite these challenges, Maharashtra's installed renewable energy capacity has grown steadily over the past decade, positioning it among the top three states in the country for non-conventional energy production."
What is the main idea of the passage?
options: A. Maharashtra has completely stopped using coal-based power
B. Maharashtra has made significant progress in renewable energy despite facing certain infrastructural and land-related challenges
C. Land acquisition disputes have made renewable energy impossible in Maharashtra
D. Private investment in Maharashtra is declining every year
explanation: The passage highlights Maharashtra's steady progress in renewable energy while acknowledging the transmission and land acquisition challenges that persist.

subject: english
question: Based on the same passage on Maharashtra's renewable energy, which two factors are cited as slowing down the pace of renewable energy projects?
options: A. Lack of government policy and low rainfall
B. Coal shortages and high electricity demand
C. Inconsistent transmission infrastructure and land acquisition disputes
D. Foreign investment restrictions and currency fluctuations
explanation: The passage explicitly states that inconsistent transmission infrastructure and land acquisition disputes slow the pace of renewable energy projects.

subject: english
question: In the passage, the phrase "non-conventional energy production" most nearly means:
options: A. Energy produced through traditional coal and thermal methods
B. Energy produced from renewable sources such as wind and solar
C. Energy that is unreliable and inefficient
D. Energy imported from other countries
explanation: In context, "non-conventional energy" refers to renewable sources like wind and solar, as opposed to conventional coal-based thermal power.

subject: english
question:
Read the passage and answer the question below:
"Mahatma Jotirao Phule was a pioneering social reformer of nineteenth-century Maharashtra who dedicated his life to the education of girls and the upliftment of oppressed castes. Along with his wife Savitribai Phule, he established one of the first schools for girls in Pune in 1848, defying strong social opposition. Phule believed that education was the key to breaking the chains of caste-based discrimination and founded the Satyashodhak Samaj in 1873 to fight for the rights of peasants and lower castes. His work laid the foundation for later social reform movements in the region."
Who did Jotirao Phule establish one of the first schools for girls with, and where?
explanation: The passage states that Phule, along with his wife Savitribai Phule, established one of the first girls' schools in Pune in 1848.

subject: english
question: According to the passage, what organization did Phule found in 1873, and for what purpose?
options: A. Prarthana Samaj, to promote monotheism
B. Satyashodhak Samaj, to fight for the rights of peasants and lower castes
C. Arya Samaj, to reform Vedic practices
D. Deccan Education Society, to promote higher education
explanation: The passage clearly states Phule founded the Satyashodhak Samaj in 1873 to fight for the rights of peasants and lower castes.

subject: english
question: What can be inferred from the passage about the social environment Phule worked in?
options: A. Society readily welcomed education for girls and lower castes
B. There was strong social resistance to educating girls and oppressed castes, which Phule had to overcome
C. The government fully funded Phule's schools without any opposition
D. Caste-based discrimination did not exist in nineteenth-century Maharashtra
explanation: The passage notes Phule established the school "defying strong social opposition," implying resistance to his reformist efforts.

subject: english
question:
Read the passage and answer the question below:
"A recent survey conducted across several urban and rural districts revealed a worrying decline in reading habits among young adults, attributed largely to the increasing use of smartphones and social media. While digital platforms have made information more accessible than ever, the survey found that sustained, deep reading of books and long-form articles has decreased significantly. Librarians interviewed for the study suggested that libraries need to reinvent themselves as community spaces offering diverse activities, rather than merely serving as repositories of books, in order to attract younger patrons back to reading."
What does the survey identify as the main cause of declining reading habits among young adults?
options: B. Increasing use of smartphones and social media
C. Lack of libraries in rural areas
D. Poor quality of school education
explanation: The passage attributes the decline in reading habits largely to the increasing use of smartphones and social media.

subject: english
question: According to the passage, what solution do librarians suggest to attract younger readers back to libraries?
options: A. Increasing the number of books available
B. Banning smartphone use inside libraries
C. Reinventing libraries as community spaces offering diverse activities
D. Charging membership fees to ensure serious readers only
explanation: The passage states librarians suggested libraries should become community spaces with diverse activities rather than mere book repositories.

subject: english
question: In the passage, the word "repositories" is closest in meaning to:
explanation: "Repositories" refers to places where things, in this case books, are stored or collected.

subject: english
question:
Read the passage and answer the question below:
"Maharashtra's agrarian economy is heavily dependent on the timely arrival and adequate spread of the southwest monsoon, which contributes nearly eighty percent of the state's annual rainfall. Regions such as Vidarbha and Marathwada, which lack extensive irrigation networks, are particularly vulnerable to erratic rainfall patterns, often resulting in crop failures and agrarian distress. In response, the state government has promoted watershed development programs and drought-resistant crop varieties to help farmers cope with climatic uncertainty. Nevertheless, experts argue that a more comprehensive shift towards efficient water management is necessary to ensure long-term agricultural sustainability."
Which regions does the passage identify as particularly vulnerable to erratic rainfall due to lack of irrigation networks?
explanation: The passage explicitly names Vidarbha and Marathwada as regions vulnerable to erratic rainfall owing to inadequate irrigation.

subject: english
question: According to the passage, what steps has the state government taken to help farmers cope with climatic uncertainty?
options: A. Banning the cultivation of water-intensive crops
B. Promoting watershed development programs and drought-resistant crop varieties
C. Providing direct cash transfers only
D. Relocating farmers to irrigated regions
explanation: The passage states the government has promoted watershed development programs and drought-resistant crop varieties to address climatic uncertainty.

subject: english
question: What does the passage ultimately suggest is necessary for long-term agricultural sustainability in Maharashtra?
options: A. Complete dependence on the monsoon
B. A comprehensive shift towards efficient water management
C. Reduction in the area under agriculture
D. Discontinuation of drought-resistant crop programs
explanation: The passage concludes that experts argue for a comprehensive shift towards efficient water management to ensure long-term agricultural sustainability.

subject: english
question: Choose the correctly punctuated/tensed sentence: The train ______ before we reached the station.
explanation: Past Perfect Tense ('had left') is used for an action completed before another past action ('reached').

subject: english
question: Fill in the blank with the correct form of the verb: She ______ in Mumbai since 2015.
explanation: Present Perfect Continuous Tense is used with 'since/for' to show an action that started in the past and continues to the present.

subject: english
question: Identify the correct sentence:
options: A. He is knowing the answer.
C. He is know the answer.
explanation: 'Know' is a stative verb and is not normally used in the continuous tense; simple present is correct.

subject: english
question: Select the sentence with the correct use of articles:
options: A. She is an honest officer.
B. She is a honest officer.
C. She is honest an officer.
D. She is the honest officer of department.
explanation: 'An' is used before 'honest' because the 'h' is silent and the word begins with a vowel sound.

subject: english
question: Choose the correct preposition: The candidates were divided ______ four groups for the interview.
explanation: 'Divide into' is the correct collocation meaning to split something into separate parts.

subject: english
question: Fill in the blank: The Mantralaya building is situated ______ the heart of Mumbai.
explanation: 'In the heart of' is the idiomatic preposition usage to indicate location within a central area.

subject: english
question: Choose the sentence that correctly uses the future perfect tense:
options: A. By next year, she will complete her training.
B. By next year, she will have completed her training.
C. By next year, she completes her training.
D. By next year, she has completed her training.
explanation: Future Perfect Tense ('will have completed') is used for an action that will be finished before a specific future time.

subject: english
question: Select the option with no article error: The Governor of Maharashtra addressed ______ gathering at ______ Raj Bhavan.
explanation: 'A gathering' is a general reference (indefinite, non-specific), while 'the Raj Bhavan' refers to a specific, unique building.

subject: english
question: Identify the correctly formed sentence using the past continuous tense:
options: A. While I was studying, the electricity went off.
B. While I studied, the electricity was going off.
C. While I was studying, the electricity was going off.
D. While I study, the electricity went off.
explanation: Past Continuous ('was studying') describes an ongoing past action interrupted by a shorter completed past action ('went off') in Simple Past.

subject: english
question: Choose the correct preposition to complete the sentence: The Zilla Parishad meeting is scheduled ______ Monday morning.
explanation: 'On' is used with specific days of the week, such as 'on Monday'.

subject: english
question: Select the grammatically correct sentence:
options: A. Neither of the officers were present at the meeting.
B. Neither of the officers was present at the meeting.
C. Neither of the officer was present at the meeting.
D. Neither of the officers has present at the meeting.
explanation: 'Neither' is a singular pronoun and therefore takes a singular verb ('was'), even though it is followed by a plural noun phrase.

subject: english
question: Choose the option that correctly explains the meaning of the idiom: 'To bell the cat'
options: B. To take a risky task upon oneself for the benefit of all
C. To flatter someone in power
D. To ignore a dangerous situation
explanation: 'To bell the cat' means to volunteer to perform a dangerous or risky task on behalf of a group.

subject: english
question: Select the correct meaning of the idiom: 'A bolt from the blue'
options: B. A sudden and unexpected event
explanation: 'A bolt from the blue' refers to something that happens suddenly and without any warning.

subject: english
question: What does the idiom 'To burn the midnight oil' mean?
options: B. To work or study late into the night
C. To start a fire accidentally
D. To celebrate late at night
explanation: 'To burn the midnight oil' means to work or study hard late into the night, referring to the earlier use of oil lamps.

subject: english
question: Identify the correct meaning of the idiom: 'To throw in the towel'
options: A. To clean up a mess
B. To accept defeat and give up
D. To offer help to someone
explanation: 'To throw in the towel' means to admit defeat and stop trying, an expression derived from boxing.

subject: english
question: The idiom 'To be in the doldrums' means:
options: B. To be in a state of stagnation or depression
D. To be in great danger
explanation: 'To be in the doldrums' means to be in a state of inactivity, stagnation, or low spirits.

subject: english
question: Choose the correct meaning of the idiom: 'To cut corners'
options: A. To finish a task perfectly
B. To do something in the cheapest or easiest way, often sacrificing quality
C. To take a shortcut route while travelling
D. To divide work equally among a team
explanation: 'To cut corners' means to do something in the easiest, quickest, or cheapest way, often compromising on standards.

subject: english
question: What is the meaning of the idiom 'To be at loggerheads'?
options: A. To be in complete agreement
B. To be in a strong disagreement or dispute with someone
C. To be confused about a decision
D. To be working together on a project
explanation: 'To be at loggerheads' means to be in serious disagreement or conflict with someone.

subject: english
question: Select the correct meaning of the idiom: 'To pull the wool over someone's eyes'
options: A. To help someone see clearly
B. To deceive or trick someone
C. To protect someone from harm
explanation: 'To pull the wool over someone's eyes' means to deceive or mislead someone.

subject: english
question: The idiom 'A wild goose chase' refers to:
options: B. A pointless or futile search or pursuit
D. A dangerous adventure in the wild
explanation: 'A wild goose chase' means a foolish and hopeless search for something that is unattainable or does not exist.

subject: english
question: Choose the correct meaning of the idiom: 'To show one's true colours'
options: A. To wear bright coloured clothes
B. To reveal one's real character, often a negative one
C. To paint a picture skillfully
D. To celebrate a festival with colours
explanation: 'To show one's true colours' means to reveal one's real nature or intentions, especially when previously hidden.

subject: english
question: What does the idiom 'To rest on one's laurels' mean?
options: A. To keep working hard despite past success
B. To be satisfied with past achievements and stop making further effort
C. To sleep peacefully after a hard day's work
D. To seek new challenges continuously
explanation: 'To rest on one's laurels' means to be content with past successes and not try to achieve more.

subject: english
question: Identify the correct meaning of the idiom: 'To read between the lines'
options: A. To read a text very quickly
B. To understand the hidden or implied meaning of something
C. To read only the important parts of a document
D. To translate a document into another language
explanation: 'To read between the lines' means to understand the implied or hidden meaning behind what is said or written, not just the literal words.

subject: english
question: Choose the sentence that is grammatically correct.
options: A. She has been working here since five years.
B. She has been working here for five years.
C. She is working here since five years.
D. She works here since five years.
explanation: 'For' is used with a period/duration of time (five years), while 'since' is used with a specific point in time.

subject: english
question: Identify the grammatically correct sentence.
options: A. Neither of the two candidates were selected for the post.
B. Neither of the two candidates was selected for the post.
C. Neither of the two candidates have been selected for the post.
D. Neither of the two candidates are selected for the post.
explanation: 'Neither' is a singular pronoun and always takes a singular verb, so 'was' is correct.

subject: english
question:
Select the correctly rearranged sentence from the given jumbled parts:
P. to attend the meeting
Q. the district collector
R. has instructed all officers
S. scheduled for tomorrow
explanation: The logical order is 'The district collector has instructed all officers to attend the meeting scheduled for tomorrow' (Q-R-P-S).

subject: english
question: Choose the correctly corrected sentence: 'Each of the students have submitted their assignment.'
options: A. Each of the students has submitted their assignment.
B. Each of the students have submitted his assignment.
C. Each of the students has submitted his or her assignment.
D. Each of the students had submit their assignment.
explanation: 'Each' takes a singular verb 'has', and traditionally requires a singular pronoun 'his or her' for grammatical agreement.

subject: english
question: Which sentence is grammatically correct?
options: A. I am knowing him since childhood.
B. I know him since childhood.
C. I have known him since childhood.
D. I had known him since childhood.
explanation: 'Know' is a stative verb and, with 'since' indicating an action continuing from the past to the present, the present perfect tense 'have known' is correct.

subject: english
question:
Select the correctly ordered sentence from the jumbled words:
P. the Mantralaya building
Q. is located
R. in the heart of Mumbai
S. of Maharashtra
explanation: The correct sentence reads 'The Mantralaya building of Maharashtra is located in the heart of Mumbai' (P-S-Q-R).

subject: english
question: Identify the grammatically correct sentence.
options: A. He is one of the best officer in the department.
B. He is one of the best officers in the department.
C. He is one of the best officer of the department.
D. He is one of best officers in the department.
explanation: The phrase 'one of the' must be followed by a plural noun, so 'officers' is correct.

subject: english
question: Choose the correctly corrected version of: 'The number of unemployed youth are increasing every year.'
options: A. The number of unemployed youth is increasing every year.
B. The number of unemployed youth are increased every year.
C. A number of unemployed youth is increasing every year.
D. The numbers of unemployed youth is increasing every year.
explanation: 'The number of' takes a singular verb, whereas 'A number of' takes a plural verb; here 'the number of...is increasing' is correct.

subject: english
question:
Select the correctly rearranged sentence from the jumbled parts:
P. despite heavy rainfall
Q. the farmers of Vidarbha
R. continued sowing their crops
S. in the region
explanation: The logical sequence is 'The farmers of Vidarbha continued sowing their crops despite heavy rainfall in the region' (Q-R-P-S).

subject: english
question: Which of the following sentences contains no grammatical error?
options: A. She is senior than me in the office.
B. She is more senior than me in the office.
C. She is senior to me in the office.
D. She is more senior to me in the office.
explanation: The adjective 'senior' (a comparative of Latin origin) is followed by 'to', not 'than', so 'senior to me' is correct.

subject: english
question: Choose the grammatically correct sentence.
options: A. If I was you, I would apply for that post.
B. If I were you, I would apply for that post.
C. If I am you, I would apply for that post.
D. If I would be you, I would apply for that post.
explanation: In hypothetical/unreal conditional sentences (Type 2), the subjunctive 'were' is used for all subjects, including 'I'.

subject: gk
question: भारतीय राज्यघटनेच्या मसुदा समितीचे अध्यक्ष कोण होते? / Who was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee of the Indian Constitution?
options: A. जवाहरलाल नेहरू / A. Jawaharlal Nehru
B. डॉ. बाबासाहेब आंबेडकर / B. Dr. B.R. Ambedkar
C. सरदार वल्लभभाई पटेल / C. Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel
D. डॉ. राजेंद्र प्रसाद / D. Dr. Rajendra Prasad
explanation: डॉ. बाबासाहेब आंबेडकर हे मसुदा समितीचे (Drafting Committee) अध्यक्ष होते.

subject: gk
question: भारतीय राज्यघटना कधी लागू करण्यात आली? / When did the Indian Constitution come into force?
explanation: राज्यघटना स्वीकारण्यात आली 26 नोव्हेंबर 1949 रोजी परंतु ती लागू (अंमलात) झाली 26 जानेवारी 1950 रोजी.

subject: gk
question: मूलभूत हक्कांचा उल्लेख राज्यघटनेच्या कोणत्या भागात आहे? / Fundamental Rights are mentioned in which Part of the Constitution?
options: A. भाग II / A. Part II
B. भाग III / B. Part III
C. भाग IV / C. Part IV
D. भाग V / D. Part V
explanation: मूलभूत हक्क (Fundamental Rights) हे राज्यघटनेच्या भाग III मध्ये कलम 12 ते 35 दरम्यान नमूद आहेत.

subject: gk
question: राज्यघटनेतील मार्गदर्शक तत्त्वे (Directive Principles) कोणत्या देशाच्या राज्यघटनेपासून घेण्यात आली आहेत? / The Directive Principles of State Policy were borrowed from the constitution of which country?
explanation: मार्गदर्शक तत्त्वे (Directive Principles of State Policy) ही आयर्लंडच्या राज्यघटनेपासून घेतली गेली आहेत.

subject: gk
question: भारतीय राज्यघटनेच्या कोणत्या कलमानुसार राष्ट्रपती राज्यात राष्ट्रपती राजवट लागू करू शकतात? / Under which Article can the President impose President's Rule in a State?
explanation: घटनात्मक यंत्रणा कोलमडल्यास कलम 356 अन्वये राज्यात राष्ट्रपती राजवट लागू केली जाते.

subject: gk
question: भारतीय राज्यघटनेची मूळ प्रत कोणी हस्तलिखित स्वरूपात लिहिली (सुलेखन/कॅलिग्राफी)? / Who calligraphed the original handwritten copy of the Indian Constitution?
options: A. नंदलाल बोस / A. Nandalal Bose
B. प्रेम बिहारी नारायण रायजादा / B. Prem Bihari Narain Raizada
C. राजा रवि वर्मा / C. Raja Ravi Varma
D. बेओहर राममनोहर सिन्हा / D. Beohar Rammanohar Sinha
explanation: मूळ राज्यघटनेचे हस्तलेखन (कॅलिग्राफी) प्रेम बिहारी नारायण रायजादा यांनी इटालिक शैलीत केले, तर सजावट नंदलाल बोस व त्यांच्या शांतिनिकेतन येथील शिष्यांनी केली.

subject: gk
question: 42 व्या घटनादुरुस्तीद्वारे (1976) राज्यघटनेच्या प्रास्ताविकेत (Preamble) कोणते शब्द समाविष्ट करण्यात आले? / Which words were added to the Preamble by the 42nd Amendment (1976)?
options: A. सार्वभौम, प्रजासत्ताक / A. Sovereign, Republic
B. समाजवादी, धर्मनिरपेक्ष, एकात्मता / B. Socialist, Secular, Integrity
C. लोकशाही, न्याय / C. Democratic, Justice
D. स्वातंत्र्य, समता / D. Liberty, Equality
explanation: 42 व्या घटनादुरुस्तीने प्रास्ताविकेत 'समाजवादी' (Socialist), 'धर्मनिरपेक्ष' (Secular) आणि 'एकात्मता' (Integrity) हे शब्द समाविष्ट केले.

subject: gk
question: घटक राज्यांच्या यादीत (State List) महाराष्ट्रासह इतर राज्यांची पुनर्रचना कोणत्या कायद्याद्वारे 1 मे 1960 रोजी झाली आणि महाराष्ट्र राज्य अस्तित्वात आले? / Under which act, effective 1 May 1960, was the state of Maharashtra formed?
options: A. राज्य पुनर्रचना कायदा 1956 / A. States Reorganisation Act 1956
B. मुंबई पुनर्रचना कायदा 1960 / B. Bombay Reorganisation Act 1960
C. भारत सरकार कायदा 1935 / C. Government of India Act 1935
D. संविधान (सातवी दुरुस्ती) कायदा 1956 / D. Constitution (Seventh Amendment) Act 1956
explanation: मुंबई पुनर्रचना कायदा (Bombay Reorganisation Act), 1960 अन्वये द्विभाषिक मुंबई राज्याचे विभाजन होऊन 1 मे 1960 रोजी महाराष्ट्र व गुजरात ही स्वतंत्र राज्ये अस्तित्वात आली.

subject: gk
question: राज्यघटनेतील कोणते कलम समान नागरी संहितेसाठी (Uniform Civil Code) राज्याला मार्गदर्शक तत्त्व म्हणून सांगते? / Which Article directs the State to secure a Uniform Civil Code?
explanation: कलम 44 हे राज्याला संपूर्ण भारतात नागरिकांसाठी समान नागरी संहिता (Uniform Civil Code) लागू करण्याचा प्रयत्न करण्याचे मार्गदर्शक तत्त्व सांगते.

subject: gk
question: राज्यघटना दुरुस्तीची प्रक्रिया राज्यघटनेच्या कोणत्या कलमात नमूद आहे? / Which Article of the Constitution lays down the procedure for amendment of the Constitution?
explanation: कलम 368 मध्ये राज्यघटना दुरुस्तीची (Amendment) प्रक्रिया व संसदेचे अधिकार नमूद केले आहेत.

subject: gk
question: 'संविधानाचा आत्मा' (Soul of the Constitution) असे कोणत्या कलमाला डॉ. आंबेडकरांनी संबोधले? / Which Article did Dr. Ambedkar call the 'Soul of the Constitution' / 'heart' of it?
explanation: घटनात्मक उपायांचा हक्क देणारे कलम 32 यास डॉ. आंबेडकरांनी 'राज्यघटनेचा आत्मा व हृदय' (Heart and Soul of the Constitution) असे संबोधले होते.

subject: gk
question: पंचायत राज व्यवस्थेला घटनात्मक दर्जा कोणत्या घटनादुरुस्तीने देण्यात आला? / Which Constitutional Amendment gave constitutional status to the Panchayati Raj system?
explanation: 73 व्या घटनादुरुस्तीने (1992) पंचायत राज संस्थांना घटनात्मक दर्जा देऊन भाग IX व अकरावी अनुसूची जोडली.

subject: gk
question: भारताच्या राष्ट्रीय उत्पन्नाचा (National Income) अंदाज कोणती संस्था प्रकाशित करते? / Which organization publishes the estimates of India's National Income?
options: A. भारतीय रिझर्व्ह बँक / Reserve Bank of India
B. राष्ट्रीय सांख्यिकी कार्यालय (NSO) / National Statistical Office (NSO)
explanation: राष्ट्रीय सांख्यिकी कार्यालय (NSO), सांख्यिकी व कार्यक्रम अंमलबजावणी मंत्रालयाच्या अंतर्गत, भारताच्या राष्ट्रीय उत्पन्नाचे अधिकृत अंदाज प्रकाशित करते.

subject: gk
question: भारतातील पहिली पंचवार्षिक योजना कोणत्या क्षेत्रावर भर देत होती? / The First Five Year Plan of India laid emphasis on which sector?
options: C. कृषी व सिंचन / Agriculture and irrigation
explanation: पहिली पंचवार्षिक योजना (1951-56) हॅरॉड-डोमर प्रतिकृतीवर आधारित असून कृषी व सिंचनाला प्राधान्य देण्यात आले होते.

subject: gk
question: 'हरित क्रांती' (Green Revolution) प्रामुख्याने कोणत्या पिकाशी संबंधित होती? / The 'Green Revolution' was primarily associated with which crop?
explanation: 1960 च्या दशकात एम. एस. स्वामिनाथन यांच्या नेतृत्वाखाली हरित क्रांती प्रामुख्याने गहू व तांदूळ या पिकांच्या उच्च उत्पादक जातींशी संबंधित होती, त्यात गहू हे सर्वाधिक यशस्वी पीक ठरले.

subject: gk
question: भारतात वस्तू व सेवा कर (GST) कोणत्या तारखेपासून लागू करण्यात आला? / From which date was the Goods and Services Tax (GST) implemented in India?
explanation: 101 व्या घटनादुरुस्तीनंतर वस्तू व सेवा कर (GST) संपूर्ण भारतात 1 जुलै 2017 पासून लागू करण्यात आला.

subject: gk
question: भारतीय रिझर्व्ह बँकेची (RBI) स्थापना कोणत्या वर्षी झाली? / In which year was the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) established?
explanation: भारतीय रिझर्व्ह बँकेची स्थापना 1 एप्रिल 1935 रोजी रिझर्व्ह बँक ऑफ इंडिया कायदा 1934 अंतर्गत झाली.

subject: gk
question: चलनवाढ (Inflation) नियंत्रित करण्यासाठी RBI कोणते धोरण साधन प्रामुख्याने वापरते? / Which policy tool does the RBI primarily use to control inflation?
options: C. प्राप्तिकर दर / Income tax rate
explanation: RBI चलनवाढ नियंत्रित करण्यासाठी रेपो दरात बदल करून पतपुरवठा व व्याजदरांवर नियंत्रण ठेवते, हे चलनविषयक धोरणाचे (Monetary Policy) प्रमुख साधन आहे.

subject: gk
question: भारतातील नियोजन आयोगाच्या (Planning Commission) जागी 2015 मध्ये कोणती संस्था स्थापन करण्यात आली? / Which body replaced the Planning Commission of India in 2015?
options: C. आर्थिक सल्लागार परिषद / Economic Advisory Council
D. राष्ट्रीय विकास परिषद / National Development Council
explanation: 1 जानेवारी 2015 रोजी नियोजन आयोगाच्या जागी निती आयोग (National Institution for Transforming India) स्थापन करण्यात आले.

subject: gk
question: स्थूल देशांतर्गत उत्पादनात (GDP) महाराष्ट्राचे स्थान भारतातील राज्यांमध्ये कसे आहे? / What is Maharashtra's position among Indian states in terms of Gross State Domestic Product (GSDP)?
explanation: महाराष्ट्र हे भारतातील सर्वाधिक स्थूल राज्य उत्पन्न (GSDP) असलेले राज्य असून देशाच्या अर्थव्यवस्थेत सर्वाधिक योगदान देणारे राज्य आहे.

subject: gk
question: भारतातील 1991 च्या आर्थिक सुधारणांचे (Economic Reforms) प्रमुख वैशिष्ट्य कोणते नव्हते? / Which was NOT a key feature of India's 1991 economic reforms?
explanation: 1991 च्या आर्थिक सुधारणा या उदारीकरण, खाजगीकरण व जागतिकीकरण (LPG) या तत्त्वांवर आधारित होत्या, संपूर्ण राष्ट्रीयीकरण हे त्याच्या विरुद्ध धोरण आहे.

subject: gk
question: राष्ट्रीय ग्रामीण रोजगार हमी कायदा (MGNREGA) कोणत्या वर्षी अंमलात आणला गेला? / In which year was the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA) implemented?
explanation: राष्ट्रीय ग्रामीण रोजगार हमी कायदा 2005 मध्ये संमत झाला व त्याची अंमलबजावणी 2006 पासून सुरू झाली, नंतर त्याचे नाव महात्मा गांधी राष्ट्रीय ग्रामीण रोजगार हमी कायदा (MGNREGA) असे करण्यात आले.

subject: gk
question: भारताचा 'गिनी गुणांक' (Gini Coefficient) कशाचे मोजमाप करतो? / What does India's 'Gini Coefficient' measure?
explanation: गिनी गुणांक हा 0 ते 1 (किंवा 0 ते 100) दरम्यान उत्पन्न किंवा संपत्तीच्या वितरणातील विषमता मोजण्यासाठी वापरला जाणारा सांख्यिकीय निर्देशांक आहे.

subject: gk
question: भारतात किती जैवविविधता हॉटस्पॉट्स आढळतात? / How many biodiversity hotspots are found in India?
explanation: भारतात चार जैवविविधता हॉटस्पॉट्स आहेत - हिमालय, इंडो-बर्मा, पश्चिम घाट-श्रीलंका आणि सुंदालँड / India has four biodiversity hotspots: Himalaya, Indo-Burma, Western Ghats-Sri Lanka, and Sundaland.

subject: gk
question: महाराष्ट्रातील पहिले रामसर स्थळ (आर्द्रभूमी) कोणते होते? / Which was the first Ramsar site (wetland) declared in Maharashtra?
explanation: नांदूर मधमेश्वर पक्षी अभयारण्य (नाशिक) २०१९ मध्ये महाराष्ट्रातील पहिले रामसर स्थळ म्हणून घोषित झाले / Nandur Madhameshwar Bird Sanctuary (Nashik) was declared Maharashtra's first Ramsar site in 2019.

subject: gk
question: भारतात 'प्रकल्प व्याघ्र' (Project Tiger) कोणत्या वर्षी सुरू करण्यात आला? / In which year was Project Tiger launched in India?
explanation: प्रकल्प व्याघ्र १ एप्रिल १९७३ रोजी सुरू करण्यात आला / Project Tiger was launched on 1 April 1973.

subject: gk
question: राष्ट्रीय हरित न्यायाधिकरणाची (NGT) स्थापना कोणत्या कायद्याअंतर्गत झाली? / Under which Act was the National Green Tribunal (NGT) established?
options: A. पर्यावरण संरक्षण अधिनियम १९८६ / Environment Protection Act 1986
B. राष्ट्रीय हरित न्यायाधिकरण अधिनियम २०१० / National Green Tribunal Act 2010
explanation: एनजीटीची स्थापना राष्ट्रीय हरित न्यायाधिकरण अधिनियम, २०१० अंतर्गत करण्यात आली / NGT was established under the National Green Tribunal Act, 2010.

question: ओझोन थर क्षय करणाऱ्या पदार्थांचा टप्प्याटप्प्याने वापर बंद करण्यासाठी कोणता आंतरराष्ट्रीय करार करण्यात आला? / Which international treaty targets the phase-out of ozone-depleting substances?
explanation: मॉन्ट्रियल प्रोटोकॉल (१९८७) ओझोन थर क्षय करणाऱ्या पदार्थांच्या वापरावर नियंत्रणासाठी आहे / The Montreal Protocol (1987) controls substances that deplete the ozone layer.

question: पॅरिस हवामान करार कोणत्या वर्षी स्वीकारण्यात आला? / In which year was the Paris Climate Agreement adopted?
explanation: पॅरिस करार डिसेंबर २०१५ मध्ये UNFCCC च्या COP21 परिषदेत स्वीकारण्यात आला / The Paris Agreement was adopted in December 2015 at COP21 of the UNFCCC.

question: सह्याद्री जीवावरण राखीव (Sahyadri Biosphere Reserve) कोणत्या दोन संरक्षित क्षेत्रांचा मिळून बनला आहे? / Sahyadri Biosphere Reserve comprises which two protected areas?
options: A. कोयना अभयारण्य व चांदोली राष्ट्रीय उद्यान / Koyna WLS and Chandoli NP
B. मेळघाट व ताडोबा / Melghat and Tadoba
C. बोर व पेंच / Bor and Pench
D. कर्नाळा व फणसाड / Karnala and Phansad
explanation: सह्याद्री जीवावरण राखीव कोयना वन्यजीव अभयारण्य व चांदोली राष्ट्रीय उद्यान यांनी मिळून बनले असून २०१० मध्ये अधिसूचित झाले / Sahyadri Biosphere Reserve, notified in 2010, comprises Koyna Wildlife Sanctuary and Chandoli National Park.
